vocab.meaning: cửa hàng vật lý truyền thống (đối lập với online)
vocab.example: While I occasionally browse in brick-and-mortar stores to check the quality, I usually finalize my shoe purchases online for better deals.

vocab.meaning: sự không đồng nhất về kích cỡ (giữa các thương hiệu)
vocab.example: The main deterrent for me is sizing inconsistencies, as a size 42 in one brand might feel like a 41 in another.

vocab.meaning: chính sách đổi trả dễ dàng/không gây khó khăn
vocab.example: I only shop on platforms that offer a no-quibble return policy in case the footwear doesn't fit like a glove.

vocab.meaning: nhận xét/phản hồi thực tế từ khách hàng
vocab.example: Before adding a pair of boots to my cart, I always scrutinize the customer testimonials to check for durability issues.

vocab.meaning: các phiên bản giới hạn được tung ra thị trường
vocab.example: Online shopping is the only way for me to get my hands on limited-edition releases that aren't stocked locally.

vocab.meaning: mua được món hàng với giá hời
vocab.example: By using price comparison tools, I was able to secure a bargain on some high-end running shoes last month.
The product description emphasized the breathable fabric, which is essential for the humid climate I live in.

vocab.meaning: giao diện (web/app) chậm chạp, khó sử dụng
vocab.example: I abandoned my last attempt to buy shoes online because the retailer's website had such a clunky interface.

vocab.meaning: thông tin chi tiết/cam kết về vận chuyển
vocab.example: I always review the shipping manifesto to ensure the delivery time frame aligns with my schedule.

vocab.meaning: việc mua sắm ngẫu hứng (không kế hoạch)
vocab.example: Targeted advertisements often lead me to make an impulse buy, even when I don't strictly need a new pair of loafers.
